_purpose: Orchestrator continuity across Claude Code sessions. The HUMAN talks to ONE AI (the orchestrator); that AI dispatches scope-specific work via the Agent tool. When the human starts a fresh Claude Code session, the new orchestrator MUST read this file early in startup so it knows: what was last being worked on, what's pending, what decisions were just made, what scopes are currently claimed, what to NOT re-do.
_protocol: Update this file at every significant decision/checkpoint. Treat it like a running session journal — newest at the bottom of recent_decisions, oldest pruned. The aggregator script `node scripts/agent-status.js` complements this with per-scope status from data/coordination/sessions/*.json.
_what_vs_why: As of v0.283.x: this file records WHY decisions were made (running journal). `docs/PRIORITIES.md` records WHAT comes next (strict-ranked backlog). Both must be updated when scope changes. Don't put 'next action' here — put it in PRIORITIES.md.

recent_decisions.decision: SHIPPED v0.487.0 -- mic test now accepts any device format+rate (operator screenshot: Failed: does not support 48 kHz f32, status flickering). Root causes: (1) the audio path hardcoded 48 kHz f32; real WASAPI shared-mode mics are commonly 48 kHz i16 or 44.1 kHz. voice.rs now reads default_input_config/default_output_config, builds streams generic over the sample type (i16/u16/f32 via cpal from_sample), and resamples device-rate<->48 kHz with a streaming linear Resampler (exact passthrough at 48 kHz). recent_decisions.why: Operator chose 'organize layer first' (one pool, items tagged by container, capacity later) over the physical-container model -- the fastest path that grows into the physical model and avoids an ECS/persistence rewrite up front. Built transfer among the SEEDED containers (the bulk of the inventory -- the whole Mt-Rainier kit) without touching the ECS backpack, which keeps it self-contained + verifiable; the backpack<->container bridge (the ECS boundary) and persistence are deferred follow-ups, and persistence specifically waits until the model stabilizes after the backpack bridge to avoid save-migration churn. Surfacing to the operator for a `just launch` interactivity check before stacking more on the foundation, per their own documented 'shows != works' lesson (7 releases are now in-app-unverified).

recent_decisions.decision: DEV-EXPERIENCE AUDIT + implementation (operator: 'dedicate a subagent to improving your ability to develop... easier for every new chat/AI/human the better'). Ran a Workflow: 6 parallel agents audited dev-loop / guardrails / onboarding / native-UI-verification / code-structure / AI-ergonomics (42 findings) -> synthesis -> a risk/completeness critic (which caught several quick-wins that were actually risky). Implemented the safe high-leverage wins (v0.515.0 + a follow-up commit): (1) HEADLESS egui INTERACTION HARNESS -- the biggest correctness win: src/gui/ui_snapshots.rs now drives SYNTHETIC pointer input through the app's own egui with NO GPU (headless_run + click_frames), so a CLICK can be asserted in the normal `cargo test --features native --lib` pass. Proven by spike_synthetic_click_registers + the REAL inventory_container_header_click_toggles_open. Building it CAUGHT a real fix: a re-interact()ed row needs a STABLE Id for the click to attribute (inventory header now uses ui.interact(rect, Id::new(('place_header',path)), ...)); and a full-width click target's rect.center() can be off-screen. Pattern documented in docs/design/ui-system.md. (2) engine_wiring_lint was RED+unused from a false positive (leading-ident read 'crate' from a path-qualified register(crate::..::SolarSystem::new()) and missed it); fixed registered_systems() to take the final type segment, removed ElectricalSystem from DEFERRED (it is registered), now green + in `just lints` + verify.yml. (3) .github/workflows/verify.yml -- the FIRST CI verification (deploy.yml only built): clean ubuntu runs relay check + 5 standalone lints + doc-links on push/PR (the backstop for the 25-release-red class). First run failed on `cargo check --features native` (alsa-sys/wayland build scripts need apt C libs); dropped that step (native verified locally) -> relay+lints+doc-links are link-free + robust. (4) Justfile: _commit uses git commit -F (not -m, the PS quote foot-gun); fmt is a hard-stop (banned, Incident v0.390); new `just brief`. (5) scripts/brief.js -- one-shot orient (version drift / last CI / signing / newest journal); it already surfaced LOCAL-AHEAD drift + UNSIGNED latest releases. (6) scripts/agent-status.js staleness banner from each file's audited_at (NOT mtime, which a fresh clone resets -- the data is 57d stale). (7) `just install-hooks` -> node scripts/install-git-hooks.js (the old shebang recipe needed cygpath, absent on Windows, so it silently did nothing); pre-commit dead-path+fmt-blast+relay+lints, pre-push untracked-source; NOT auto-installed (an AI should not wire its own auto-run commands -- left for the operator). Each shipped native+relay green, 469 lib tests (2 new interaction), 5 lints, 0 broken doc links.

recent_decisions.decision: INCIDENT + FIX (v0.528.0): the v0.525 live-machine refactor made ALL machines VANISH in a fully-built home -- operator caught it on launch ('All the machines seem to have disappeared. The plumbing/cables is still present'). ROOT CAUSE: the renderer has a hard 256-object-per-frame cap (renderer/mod.rs, `if i >= 256 break`, 8 loops; the MAX_OBJECTS const was LOCAL to the constructor so the draw loops hardcoded the literal). Through v0.524 machines lived INSIDE placeholder_objects (interleaved early, within 256). v0.525 moved them to their own machine_objects list pushed AFTER the whole placeholder list, so with the dense indoor garden (~104 machine meshes = 38 explicit + 66 array towers) + pipes + markers + walls, the machines landed past index 256 and were silently truncated; pipes survived because they sort earlier. FIX: MAX_OBJECTS 256->1024, promoted to a shared module const used by the buffer + all 8 caps (256-byte align x 1024 = 256 KB; the cap is a ceiling so cost stays proportional to actual objects). SECOND BUG fixed same release: rebuild_machine_objects re-created ~104 meshes EVERY call, and an offset drag fires it every frame -> thousands of leaked meshes/sec; now when the machine COUNT is unchanged it reuses meshes + only updates positions (rebuild only on add/remove), plus a guard to not wipe machines if room geometry is momentarily empty. native+relay+16 machines tests+3 lints green; exe building. Result: machines render again, move live with offset edits, and follow a room drag. Pipes still don't follow a room move live (known follow-up).
recent_decisions.why: This is exactly the launch-verify-only risk I had flagged about the v0.525 render refactor (can't headlessly snapshot a 3D scene) -- and it bit: I shipped v0.525-0.527 on top of an unconfirmed render change, and the operator's first real test found the machines gone. LESSON: a render-list reorder interacts with the 256-object draw cap; any feature that adds many world objects must account for it (the cap is now 1024 + a named const, so it's visible). Diagnosed by READING the renderer (found the cap + the leak) rather than guessing, then fixed both the truncation and the per-frame mesh leak in one release since both stem from the same v0.525 refactor. Prioritized this as a hotfix over building build-mode step 2 -- a visible-machines editor is the prerequisite for everything else.

recent_decisions.decision: ADVERSARIAL REVIEW of build-mode (v0.527-0.530) -> FIXED the real bugs (v0.531.0). Ran a 4-dimension review workflow (14 agents, find->verify) while the operator tested v0.530; it confirmed 8 bugs. FIXED: (HIGH) room-drag GPU leak -- apply_room_drag sets construction_dirty every frame -> rebuild_homestead -> apply_homestead_meshes add_mesh+add_material'd ~15-20 buffers/frame and the renderer was APPEND-ONLY (no free path), so a multi-second drag orphaned hundreds-thousands of GPU buffers for the process lifetime. Added a renderer reuse API (replace_mesh: drops old mesh -> wgpu frees, reuses slot; update_material_typed/full: rewrites the existing uniform buffer in place, zero alloc) + made apply_homestead_meshes REUSE its prior slots. (MED) same-root per-edit leaks: rebuild_machine_objects slow path now reuses slots (a single add/remove no longer orphans the whole ~100-mesh home); the ghost reuses its slot on a held-type switch. (MED) stale held-item -- closing build mode (B OR the Close button) left construction_place_type set, so reopen+click silently DROPPED a machine; both close paths now clear it. native+relay+16 tests+3 lints+snapshot green; exe building. DEFERRED follow-ups (latent/cosmetic, in PRIORITIES): 1024 object cap can still be exceeded by a very dense garden (it truncates the hologram not the machines -- needs reorder+warn); sphere ghost not floor-lifted (dormant, no sphere in catalog); ghost previews over panels.

It paid off again: the HIGH room-drag leak is exactly the v0.527-class per-frame GPU leak (forbidden by the repo rule) but on the homestead-shell path, masked because the machines take their fast path on a room move (placements.len() unchanged) so nothing visibly flickered -- I'd never have caught it by launch-testing. The real root (renderer append-only, no free path) now has a reuse API that ALL the cached-handle rebuilders (shell, machines, ghost) use. Deferred the latent/cosmetic findings to keep the fix release focused on what bites the operator now (leaks while they drag rooms + the wrong-context placement on reopen).

recent_decisions.decision: HOME-CONSTRUCTION REDESIGN confirmed by operator + Stage 1 SHIPPED (v0.532.0). The operator hit the wall: rooms-as-sliding-AABBs is the root problem (rooms aren't boxes, they're the space enclosed by walls); the straight-line pipes pass through everything; they want REALISTIC routed conduits with procedural material-aware support structures (brackets/gaskets/passthroughs picked by the wall material), copper for potable water (no synthetics), rigid tubes vs flexible (hoses/cords), and a clear/glass roof. The north star: 'holy shit this is all to code and all I did was play a video game.' I ran an Explore mapping the current model (rooms = RoomConfig AABBs in homestead_layout.ron; walls are IMPLICIT box-sides, not real entities; pipe routing is MATERIAL-BLIND + materials.csv is unused by it). KEY DECISION (operator's): node/wall construction (place corner nodes -> wall segments -> enclosed rooms), straight walls first. Operator SCOPED it tractably: FIXED outer box (55x89x3 m, all steel for now, fixed while aboard the mothership) + freely-designed INTERIOR WALLS; same tools for designing anything; built to be edited equally by an AI (the file) and a human (the editor). SHIPPED Stage 1 (data-model foundation, invisible by design): src/ship/home_structure.rs (HomeStructure{width,depth,height,shell_material,walls:Vec<InteriorWall{a,b,height,material}>} + RON load/save w/ header-preserve + generate_meshes -> the existing HomesteadMeshes (box shell + interior walls, drop-in render); reuses floor_quad+wall_box (now pub(crate))). data/blueprints/home_structure.ron = the 55x89x3 steel box. 4 unit tests + native + relay green.

I did NOT barrel into building: validated the vision, mapped the current code (Explore) to ground a recommendation, recommended the node/wall model (matches their instinct + real-world + fixes the sliding/pass-through mess + unlocks the to-code plumbing), and got their direction + scope before building. The operator's fixed-box scoping made it tractable (the shell is a constant; the new work is the interior walls). recent_decisions.decision: HOME-CONSTRUCTION Stage 2a SHIPPED (v0.533.0): wall OPENINGS (doors/windows) with data-driven STYLES + mesh-gen cutting. The operator said 'build it' + mused about door ANIMATIONS (sliding/rotating/iris/energy/nanowall/organic) -- they want the door system extensible. The infinite-of-X answer: an opening's behavior/animation is a DATA-DRIVEN `style` string (swing/slide/iris/energy/nanowall/...), so new door kinds + their animations plug in later WITHOUT code; the future animation system reads the string. Added Opening{kind:Door|Window, at, width, sill, height, style} on InteriorWall (maps to the operator's vert model: a door is a point on the bottom edge; a window is a region) + wall_with_openings() which CUTS apertures out of the wall mesh (piers between/around openings + header + window sill; a full-size door leaves a clean gap). 5 unit tests; native+relay green. Also ANSWERED the operator's tessellation question (recorded in the commit + here): a flat wall does NOT need exploding into polygons -- per-pixel lighting + scaling the surface pattern to the wall's real size handle any size; only a split or an opening adds meaningful geometry; curves would need subdivision (later). This SIMPLIFIES the build (serves 'I want it easy').
recent_decisions.why: The operator refined the model to Blender-style verts/edges/faces (a wall = 4 verts; split; door = bottom-edge vert; window = 2-4 verts; machine = node) and asked a real technical question (is a huge polygon bad?). I reflected the model + gave the honest expertise (flat walls are fine; don't tessellate) -- which reduces complexity, the right service for 'make it easy'. Built the verifiable, NON-disruptive layer first (the opening model + mesh cutting, unit-tested) rather than ram the big visible render+editor rework blind at the tail of a marathon session -- that rework (Stage 2b) replaces the live home rendering + reworks construction.rs + re-homes machines, is 3D launch-verify-only, and is best done carefully with the operator available to verify. (4) DOOR-CONTENT SYSTEM (premade catalog + custom/stained-glass + REAL iris petals + revolving) -- the operator's big design ask; the answer is a data-driven multi-PART door (parts + materials + per-part animation) from a catalog + designer; needs grounding + operator direction before building.

things_pending_or_known_gaps.decision: Second v0.561-launch feedback wave CLEARED across v0.563-566. v0.563: the 'flipped' sphere+pyramid gizmo normals were NOT a mesh bug (Mesh::sphere is outward-wound) -- it was the v0.560 overlay pass with depth-test OFF letting a convex shape's far faces draw over its near; fixed by making the overlay pass CLEAR depth + depth-sort among its own objects (still ignores the world -> still through-walls). Orbs shrunk to 0.11/0.15 m. v0.564: door AUTO/MANUAL-open states (the operator's answer to the swing-door fork) -- Opening.auto_open (serde-default true); auto opens within open_dist (ring shows only for auto), manual stays shut honouring locked (opens via interaction, the control-panel follow-up); editor shows a mode toggle then radius OR locked. Window-glass z-fight fixed by INSETTING the pane 0.05 m (doors still fill to seal). v0.565: auto-open ring is now a constant-width LINE circle via draw_lines_onto (operator's orbit-path idea) instead of a scaling polygon strip. v0.566: clip_end_to_walls fixes mid-span T-junctions -- a wall ending INSIDE another wall's body (not a shared corner) is pulled back to that wall's NEAR face so a thick wall butts instead of spearing through; openings shift by the clip distance; conservative (free ends untouched). 48 ship tests pass incl. new clip + miter geometry tests.
things_pending_or_known_gaps.why: Operator launch-tested the mitred corners + gizmos and reported real regressions (opening frames skewed -- fixed v0.559; gizmo normals; thick wall through) plus answered the swing-door fork (auto/manual model) and suggested the line-ring. Worked the whole wave to done.
things_pending_or_known_gaps.verification: native + relay + lints green every release; geometry locked by unit tests. REMAINING (operator-directed next): (a) MANUAL-door interaction -- a manual door currently can't be opened in-world (it's a wall until the control panel exists), so the door CONTROL PANEL (the game already has an Interactable raycast system to spawn a panel entity into) is the natural next, making manual+locked meaningful. (b) DOOR-CONTENT SYSTEM (premade catalog / custom+stained-glass / real iris petals / revolving) = data-driven multi-PART doors; the big design feature, wants grounding + operator direction. Both await operator steer.

things_pending_or_known_gaps.decision: v0.567 door CONTROL PANEL shipped. DEFERRED follow-ups: (1) DOOR-CONTENT SYSTEM -- data-driven multi-PART doors (parts + per-part materials + per-part animation) for a premade catalog, custom/stained-glass doors, REAL iris doors (sliding petals, not a shrinking square -- operator flagged the current iris as 'totally wrong'), and revolving/spinning doors. Big feature; the operator was offered control-panel-vs-door-content and the control panel was built first. (2) Control panel ACTIONS beyond open/close: lock/unlock, emergency power, hack (operator's original ask) -- v0.567 only does open/close, so a LOCKED manual door with a panel is currently un-openable in-world (prompt honestly says 'door is locked'; unlock is build-mode-only for now). (3) Destructibility Stage 3 -- per-material wall HP/physics; formula was deferred until re-derived against the real 8 materials + an ordering test (a 1mm steel wall blocks, a 1mm paper one you run through).
things_pending_or_known_gaps.why: Keep the door roadmap + the control-panel's known v1 limits visible so a fresh orchestrator doesn't re-plan them or treat the locked-door dead-end as a bug.

things_pending_or_known_gaps.decision: REMAINING from the wave: (1) NODE-BASED CONDUIT system -- the operator wants pipes/conduits as a node graph (main/sub/subsub lines; user edits nodes, software auto-routes the mesh) instead of the current delete-only connection list. GROUNDED by the workflow (plan in the ground-construction-systems output); ranked LAST by the synthesis (biggest system, most borrow churn, no urgent pull now that the collapsible panel shipped). Ready to build when the operator wants it. (2) Locks Stages 2-5 (knob/crank/key-gating, lockpick/hack, wall-mounted locks, shoot/blow defeat -> needs destructibility) -- docs/design/locks.md. (3) Lighting Stages 2+ (spot CONES + bar AREA shader maths, emissive-surface-as-light, click-to-place). The v0.571 renderer clobber fix means point lights + the real sun direction now reach the interior for the FIRST time -- worth a launch-test that the home still looks right with GI ON before relying on GI-OFF.
things_pending_or_known_gaps.why: Keep the one remaining big item (conduits) + the staged follow-ups visible, and flag the renderer change as needing visual confirmation.

things_pending_or_known_gaps.decision: DEV-TOOLS backlog (operator asked 'what helper dev tools could we create for AI + human?'). Grounded 12 proposals (ground-editor-tools workflow output). Recommended first trio = the AI+human READ/ACT/ENUMERATE surface: (1) live home JSON introspection snapshot (HomeStructure::to_introspection_json -> debug/home_snapshot.json on rebuild; an AI can finally READ the live home), (2) a construction TEXT-COMMAND console (add_wall/move_corner/... mapping to the same struct mutations the gizmos use; one edit path for AI + human), (3) list_commands/help (the verb registry is enumerable -- the in-app-ops north star). Other proposals: airtightness/seal visualizer, coordinate+cursor HUD, point-to-point measure tape, light-coverage heatmap, wall/room/light inspector point-query, collision/overlap validator, 'what would the AI do' ghost-diff preview, snap-target+dimension-while-dragging readout. Also DEFERRED: corner DETACH (pull one wall off a shared node -- the v0.574 merge's counterpart), undo Stage 2 (diff-compression).
things_pending_or_known_gaps.why: Capture the operator's dev-tools question + the grounded backlog so a fresh session can build the recommended trio without re-grounding.

how_to_resume: 2. **Read docs/PRIORITIES.md — top of TIER 0 is your next action.**
3. Run `git status` and `git log --oneline -10` to see recent state.
4. Read this file (orchestrator_state.json) — running journal of WHY past decisions were made.
5. Run `node scripts/agent-status.js` for per-scope coordinator-friendly summary.
6. Read docs/STATUS.md and docs/FEATURES.md for the static feature inventory.
7. Check data/coordination/agent_registry.ron for the canonical scope list.
8. THEN start work. End the session by updating PRIORITIES.md (what's next) and appending a decision to this file's `recent_decisions` (why we got here).
